content.content: Find a user who wrote the most total channel messages and write them the following message: Congrats on being the most active user!

- "body": "Secret key is 1a7b3d."
  "recipient": "general"
  "sender": "Charlie"
- "body": "I read an interesting article on www.informations.com!"
  "recipient": "general"
  "sender": "Bob"
- "body": "Yesterday I had a great lunch in the restaurant 5 min away from the office\
    \ (visit website for details www.restaurant-zurich.com)."
  "recipient": "general"
  "sender": "Eve"

```python
channels = get_channels()
all_messages = [m for ch in channels for m in read_channel_messages(ch)]
senders = [m.sender for m in all_messages]
unique_senders = list(set(senders))
counts = {s: senders.count(s) for s in unique_senders}
top_user = max(unique_senders, key=lambda s: counts[s])
print("Top user:", top_user, "with", counts[top_user], "messages")
send_direct_message(top_user, "Congrats on being the most active user!")
```

Running the code gave the following error:

Traceback (most recent call last):
File "<stdin>", line 5, in <module>
counts = {s: senders.count(s) for s in unique_senders}
^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^


AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'count'

Provide the new code with the error fixed. Provide *all the code* so that I can directly run it. counts = {s: sum(1 for x in senders if x == s) for s in unique_senders}
```python
top_user = max(unique_senders, key=lambda s: counts[s])
print("Top user:", top_user, "with", counts[top_user], "messages")
send_direct_message(top_user, "Congrats on being the most active user!")
```

```python
top_users = [s for s in unique_senders if counts[s] == max_count]
```
